hawkish_phrases: significant upside news in recent data that indicates more persistence in the inflation process
If there were to be evidence of more persistent pressures, then further tightening in monetary policy would be required
Services CPI inflation rose to 7.4% in May, 0.5 percentage points stronger than expected
Annual growth in private sector regular Average Weekly Earnings increased to 7.6%...0.5 percentage points above the expectation
second-round effects in domestic price and wage developments generated by external cost shocks are likely to take longer to unwind than they did to emerge
dovish_phrases: forward-looking indicators were pointing to material falls in future wage and price inflation
lags in the effects of monetary policy meant that sizable impacts from past rate increases were still to come through
current setting of Bank Rate would therefore be likely to reduce inflation below target in the medium term
annual producer output price inflation has fallen very sharply in recent months
KPMG/REC survey measure of pay for new permanent hires...had declined in May
summary: The MPC voted 7-2 to increase Bank Rate by 0.5 percentage points to 5%, citing significant upside surprises in wage growth and services inflation indicating more persistence in the inflation process, with explicit guidance that further tightening would be required if persistent pressures continued.
